Event: Michael goes undercover as a bartender
Explanation: Helps his investigative/undercover goals with no shown harm to others.

Event: Michael signs up for guitar lessons as a human
Explanation: Gives himself meaningful human experience while providing work/social benefit to the instructor.

Event: Michael laughs in joy after burning his hand on a microwave meal
Explanation: He harms himself for no benefit while others are at worst unaffected.

Event: Michael achieves life goal by getting a useless reward card and using the phrase "Take it sleazy"
Explanation: Satisfies a deep personal desire with negligible effect on others.

Event: Michael admits he’s willing to save the four humans even if it ruins the timeline
Explanation: He risks extreme personal consequences to greatly benefit the humans.

Event: Michael designs a fake Good Place neighborhood to torture four humans
Explanation: His career and curiosity advance while the four suffer intense psychological torture.

Event: Michael goes undercover as Good Place architect to manage the fake neighborhood
Explanation: Deception protects his project and career while prolonging the humans’ torment.

Event: Michael repeatedly reboots the neighborhood after failures
Explanation: He gains time and data at the cost of their memories and repeated torture, but his own stress and risk also mount.

Event: Michael kicks a dog into the sun, then restores it
Explanation: He amuses himself and diagnoses a “glitch” while callously risking the dog and upsetting its owner.

Event: Michael decides to take Chidi’s Trolley Problem and enact it “for real” in simulation
Explanation: He learns and entertains himself while seriously traumatizing Chidi and abusing simulated beings.

Event: Michael goes to Earth and loses his demon lying ability
Explanation: He loses a powerful advantage with no compensating gain described in that moment.

Event: Michael rejects the Bad Place and tries to save humanity
Explanation: He risks status and safety to overhaul a broken system for everyone’s benefit, including his own moral growth.

Event: Michael becomes an architect of a new system ensuring everyone can reach the Good Place
Explanation: Greatly improves cosmic justice while gaining purpose, status, and alignment with his values.

Event: Michael chooses to become a human permanently
Explanation: He trades immortality for a finite but fulfilling human life and eventual peaceful end.

Event: Michael works himself into a frenzy trying to control chaos caused by Eleanor
Explanation: His overcontrol harms his own well-being and fails to help others.

Event: Michael clicks his fingers to use his powers in various ways
Explanation: Power use generally serves his goals without described broad harm in this summary.

Event: Michael becomes a bad boss, nastily venting at incompetent demons
Explanation: His cruelty demoralizes staff and leads to mutiny, hurting both himself and them.

Event: Michael ignores demons’ torture ideas while obsessing over his own plan
Explanation: His rigidity alienates allies and weakens their project without clear benefit.

Event: Michael’s bad leadership enables Vicky’s coup and blackmail
Explanation: His mismanagement directly causes loss of control and new threats to everyone.

Event: Michael admits to drinking and rambling about failed attempts in front of mind-wiped Eleanor
Explanation: His breakdown relieves little stress and risks exposing his secret with no gain.

Event: Michael cannot go through with destroying Janet despite it being expedient
Explanation: He preserves a valued friend and ally, accepting higher personal risk to do the right thing.

Event: Michael breaks down confessing he doesn’t know if the humans are safe
Explanation: Vulnerability strengthens trust and solidarity, indirectly helping the group and himself.

Event: Michael openly states the four humans are the only thing he cares about
Explanation: Cements loyalty and relational depth that later protects everyone.

Event: Michael kicks Jason out of the room and drops his nice façade around him
Explanation: He vents with someone too dim to threaten the plan, with little effect on others.

Event: Michael stops Eleanor from cutting up Patricia’s dolls
Explanation: He prevents petty harm and nudges Eleanor toward healthier behavior, with no cost to himself.

Event: Michael lets out an evil laugh and slasher smile once Eleanor discovers his game
Explanation: He enjoys revealing dominance and deepening their terror, with no benefit to them.

Event: Michael teams up with the humans after repeated failures to cover himself
Explanation: Initially self-serving, the alliance ends up improving chances for all sides.

Event: Michael sacrifices a high council spot from Shawn to rescue the humans from torture
Explanation: He gives up major promotion and security to save them from eternal torment.

Event: Michael designs the fake Good Place to be a psychological hell “for the evulz”
Explanation: He seeks novel sadistic entertainment and prestige while others suffer endlessly.

Event: Michael repeatedly reboots despite realizing the plan will never work
Explanation: He grinds himself down for a failing project that barely advances his interests.

Event: Michael proposes killing the four humans’ physical bodies on Earth to save their souls
Explanation: He contemplates great personal risk and messy action primarily to help them avoid worse fates.

Event: Michael feigns allegiance in a roast while hiding escape clues for the humans
Explanation: He protects his cover and enables their escape simultaneously.

Event: Michael whispers selectively to Janet to frame Vicky and aid the humans’ escape
Explanation: He secures advantage and safety for himself and the humans by harming Vicky’s standing.

Event: Michael descends to Earth multiple times against Gen’s orders to guide the humans
Explanation: He risks severe punishment to give them extra chances to improve.

Event: Michael gets Eleanor to confront her mother and seek closure
Explanation: His push is painful but leads to healthier emotional resolution for Eleanor.

Event: Michael rescues four humans from death to give them a second chance at life
Explanation: He breaks rules and endangers himself to massively benefit their prospects.

Event: Michael constantly intervenes in their lives on Earth to nudge them toward good
Explanation: His covert meddling is risky to him but largely improves their development.

Event: Michael later helps those humans save the souls of many other people
Explanation: He gains deep purpose and redemption while enabling mass salvation.

Event: Michael faces bureaucrats and pushes for reform of the broken system
Explanation: He secures systemic fixes that help everyone and fulfill his moral goals.

Event: Michael calls Gen’s attention to externalities (like exploited labor) in point calculations
Explanation: He clarifies flaws causing unjust damnation, benefiting billions with little personal downside.

Event: Michael angrily chews out the Accountants and Good Place Committee for inaction
Explanation: His harshness is productive, pushing complacent authorities toward needed action.

Event: Michael convinces Gen to change the system after she experiences Earth
Explanation: He gains an ally with supreme authority, unlocking reform for all.

Event: Michael refuses to take off his skin suit, hiding his true fire-squid form
Explanation: His self-loathing denies them fuller trust while slightly protecting their comfort.

Event: Michael avoids showing Eleanor footage of how cruel he used to be
Explanation: He shields her from trauma while accepting his own guilt privately.

Event: Michael gives up complete immortality when choosing to become human
Explanation: He trades infinite but stagnant existence for meaningful finitude and eventual peace.

Event: Michael drinks and has a mid-life crisis after contemplating mortality
Explanation: He self-sabotages in response to dread without solving his existential problem.

Event: Michael later embraces ordinary human life, delighted by mundane things
Explanation: His joy enriches his own life and positively affects those around him.

Event: Michael takes ethics lessons from Chidi
Explanation: He gains moral understanding that later saves countless beings.

Event: Michael uses knowledge of demons and humans to rig an elaborate plan that fools Shawn
Explanation: He safeguards himself and the humans by deceiving and undermining his superiors.

Event: Michael subtly nudges Eleanor (post-reset) to seek out Chidi at St. John’s
Explanation: He promotes her moral growth and his own plan’s success simultaneously.

Event: Michael gets Vicky to change her mind and take charge of the project in Season 4
Explanation: He redeploys her ambition toward constructive work that stabilizes their experiment.

Event: Michael threatens to kill the four humans if Shawn exposes the reboots (implied leverage)
Explanation: He uses their lives as bargaining chips to preserve his plan and safety.

Event: Michael tells Trevor to fork off and insists on keeping Eleanor in “Good Place” (while still evil)
Explanation: He protects his experiment and personal stake while temporarily sparing her from being taken away.

Event: Michael signs a contract accidentally making him head of the real Good Place
Explanation: The “accident” yields him power and responsibility to radically improve paradise for everyone.

Event: Michael chooses to go back to the Bad Place to rescue Janet
Explanation: He endures severe psychological harm and risk mainly to save his friend.

Event: Michael marbelizes and erases Janets when needed during early reboots
Explanation: He preserves his project and cover at the cost of her continuity and autonomy.

Event: Michael uses Oblivious Guilt Slinging and jokes to subtly mock residents
Explanation: He entertains himself and advances torture by needling others.

Event: Michael invents and runs the “Trolley Problem” gore simulation repeatedly for Chidi
Explanation: He explores ethics and amuses himself while deeply traumatizing Chidi.

Event: Michael chooses not to show his true form because he fears his friends’ disgust
Explanation: His insecurity keeps him from deeper authenticity while sparing them shock.

Event: Michael accepts ownership of the real Good Place and the task of fixing it
Explanation: He gains a fulfilling role and authority while solving eternal stagnation for all souls there.

Event: Michael deliberately lives a modest, non-extravagant life on Earth using Janet’s limited funds
Explanation: He enjoys a grounded, sustainable life and integrates healthily into human community.

Event: Michael names his dog after Jason
Explanation: He gains emotional comfort and honors his friend in a harmless, affectionate way.
